learning_outcomes.description: Read a range of passages based on parts of speech and syntax which are covered in the JACT Reading Greek course
Translate into clear English a range of passages in the JACT Reading Greek course
Recognise and classify a range of different parts of speech
Parse (viz. give the grammatical properties of) various parts of speech which are covered in the JACT Reading Greek course.

assessments.due_date: There are 5 grammar exercises in GREK2120. Each exercise is worth 5% and together will constitute 25% of the final grade. The Course Program on Blackboard lists the week in which each grammar exercise is due. The assignment must be submitted on or before Friday 2pm of the relevant week. In general, a grammar exercise will have to be submitted every fortnight. The exercise will be made available on Blackboard and you must upload the answers to Blackboard as well before the stated due date.
assessments.category: Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Quiz, Translation/ Interpretation
assessments.task_description:
Students will be set 5 grammar exercises throughout the course, based on the exercises contained in the Reading Greek Grammar book. Each exercise is worth 5% of the final mark for a total of 25%.

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date: The exam will be held in class on Wednesday 9 September (Week 7)
assessments.task_description: The in-semester test will consist of a variety of parsing, grammatical and translation questions marked out of 25. It will cover the passages studied in Reading Greek Text and Grammar, Section 7A-9E.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description: The end-of-semester exam will consist of a variety of parsing, grammatical and translation questions marked out of 50. It will cover the passages studied in Reading Greek Text and Grammar, Section 7A-11C.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
